text.title: Chunking Basics — Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules for chunking: tiktoken measurement, default 500/50 band, content-type routing, metadata at creation, content-based IDs, version on strategy change.

rule.statement: Token counts MUST be measured with the same tiktoken encoding as the target embedding model (e.g. cl100k_base for OpenAI text-embedding-3-*). Word counts MUST NOT be used as a token proxy.

rationale.source: Pinecone chunking guide + OpenAI tokenizer docs

rule.rationale: Word counts diverge from token counts by ~30% for English and far more for non-Latin scripts. Chunk_size enforced in words can produce chunks that exceed the embedding model's input cap.

rule.statement: Start with chunk_size=500 tokens, overlap=50 tokens. Use 1000–1500 for legal/technical, 200–400 for QA. Tune only after the first retrieval benchmark on ≥5 queries.
rule.rationale: 500 tokens is the empirical middle ground that preserves context while keeping chunks focused. Tuning without a benchmark is guesswork.

rule.statement: Different content types MUST route to different splitters: code goes to AST splitter, markdown goes to header splitter, html goes to tag splitter, prose goes to recursive/sentence splitter. The pipeline MUST detect type before applying the splitter.
rule.rationale: A prose splitter on code destroys function boundaries; a code splitter on prose creates incoherent chunks. Strategy must match structure.

rule.statement: Overlap MUST be 10–20% of chunk_size. For QA corpora where questions span chunk boundaries use the upper 20% bound.

rationale.source: Retrieval recall studies on overlapping windows

rule.rationale: Zero overlap causes boundary-spanning queries to retrieve no relevant chunk. Overlap above 20% inflates index size with diminishing recall gain.

rule.statement: Each chunk MUST carry source, page, chunk_index, strategy, version, token_count at creation. Retrofitting metadata after indexing requires a full re-index.
rule.rationale: Missing source/page makes hits unattributable; missing strategy/version makes A/B comparisons impossible.

rule.statement: Chunk IDs MUST be content-based: md5(source_path + chunk_index + strategy_version). Sequential integers MUST NOT be used.
rule.rationale: Sequential IDs collide on update or re-chunk. Content-based IDs keep re-ingest idempotent and allow incremental delete/replace.
